---
title: "COMETA — Cocoa Metabolomics"
description: "COMETA (COcoa METAbolomics) was a six-year interdisciplinary research initiative"
image: untitled.jpg
date: "01-02-2016"
categories: [PhD]
---

![Photo by [Trnava University](https://unsplash.com/photos/gold-and-blue-trophy-on-white-table-SwCMZwrhQm8?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText)](untitled.jpg){width="50%" target="_blank"}

COMETA (COcoa METAbolomics) was a six-year interdisciplinary research initiative between Jacobs University Bremen and Barry Callebaut, aimed at mapping the roughly 20,000 molecules that give cocoa its taste, aroma, and flavor — from raw bean to finished chocolate. I joined the project in 2016 as a PhD student, contributing to two areas of the research: kinetic modelling of the fermentation process, and statistical consulting on a related metabolomics study. [Learn more about COMETA](http://mullrich.user.jacobs-university.de/cocoa-metabolomics/){target="_blank"}.
